methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: The total minted emission in an epoch must exactly equal the sum of the rebase amount, team emissions, and gauge rewards.
invariants.attack_scenario: If emissions exceed the sum of distributions, excess tokens are minted and trapped or stolen. If distributions exceed emissions, the transaction reverts or drains other reserves, breaking the protocol's inflation schedule.

invariants.description: The total supply of staked votes in a Bribe contract must exactly equal the sum of all individual staked balances.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the total supply is less than the sum of individual balances, the reward per token calculation will be artificially inflated, allowing attackers to drain the bribe contract's reward tokens.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_bribe_supply() public view { assert(totalSupply == sum_balances); }

invariants.description: The global reward index in GaugeManager must be monotonically increasing to prevent yield theft.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the index can be manipulated to decrease, the delta calculation for gauge claimable amounts would underflow or incorrectly reduce claimable rewards, stealing yield from Liquidity Providers.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_index_monotonicity() public view { assert(index >= old_index); }

invariants.description: Claimed fees from the Algebra pool must be fully distributed between the dibs (referral) address and the internal bribe contract without leaving dust in the GaugeCL contract.
invariants.attack_scenario: If fees are not fully distributed, they accumulate as trapped dust in the GaugeCL contract, leading to a permanent loss of yield for voters and liquidity providers.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_fee_distribution() public view { assert(claimed == dibs_fee + bribe_fee); }

invariants.description: When a gauge is killed, its claimable rewards must be reset to zero and returned to the minter.
invariants.attack_scenario: If a killed gauge retains its claimable balance, malicious or deprecated gauges could still distribute emissions, wasting protocol inflation and incentivizing dead pools.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_killed_gauge_claimable() public view { if(!isAlive[gauge]) assert(claimable[gauge] == 0); }

invariants.description: The circulating supply calculation must exactly equal the total supply minus the balance of the ve contract and the burn address.
invariants.attack_scenario: If circulating supply is miscalculated, the rebase amount will be incorrect, leading to either hyperinflation or unfair dilution of veNFT holders.
